design_summary: End-to-end compilation from .axm source to native executable binary. The axiom compiler already generates LLVM IR text; M1.5 adds the final step: writing the IR to a temp file and invoking an external C compiler (clang or cl.exe) to produce a native binary. On this Windows/MSVC system, we use clang (preferred) or fall back to cl.exe via the Visual Studio vcvars64 environment. The main new code is ~120 lines in axiom-driver/src/main.rs plus a new ~180-line module axiom-driver/src/compile.rs that encapsulates compiler discovery and invocation.

available_c_compilers.clang: NOT on PATH — must be installed separately or found by searching common locations
available_c_compilers.gcc: NOT on PATH — no MSYS2/MinGW installation detected
available_c_compilers.cl_exe: Available via Visual Studio 18 Community at C:/Program Files/Microsoft Visual Studio/18/Community/VC/Tools/MSVC/14.44.35207/bin/Hostx64/x64/cl.exe and 14.50.35717
available_c_compilers.link_exe: Available via Visual Studio at C:/Program Files/Microsoft Visual Studio/18/Community/VC/Tools/MSVC/14.44.35207/bin/Hostx64/x64/link.exe

environment_analysis.compiler_strategy: Use Rust's `cc` crate for compiler discovery (it handles MSVC vcvars setup automatically). As a fallback, search PATH for clang, then invoke cl.exe via vcvars64.bat shell wrapper. The `cc` crate is the most robust approach because it uses the same MSVC discovery logic as Cargo itself.

design_decisions.choice: Use the Rust `cc` crate for C compiler discovery and invocation
rationale: The `cc` crate automatically discovers MSVC installations, sets up vcvars environment, and handles all Windows SDK path complexity
It uses the same MSVC detection as cargo/rustc itself, so if `cargo build` works, `cc` will find a compiler
Cross-platform: works on Linux (finds gcc/clang), macOS (finds clang), Windows (finds cl.exe)
Eliminates need to manually handle vcvars64.bat, Windows SDK paths, and MSVC version discovery
Well-maintained, widely-used crate (used by thousands of Rust projects)
tradeoffs: Adds a runtime dependency on `cc` crate (but it is lightweight and pure Rust)
The `cc` crate is primarily designed for build scripts, but its compiler discovery API works fine at runtime too

design_decisions.choice: Use std::process::Command with direct clang/gcc invocation as primary path, cc crate as MSVC fallback
rationale: clang can compile .ll files directly to executables in a single invocation: `clang -O2 foo.ll -o foo`
This is simpler and more transparent than going through the cc crate for the happy path
When clang is not available (as on this system), fall back to a two-step MSVC approach: use clang from LLVM or use `cc` crate to compile .ll via cl.exe
On Windows without clang: write .ll, use system `llc` to produce .obj, then use MSVC link.exe to link
Since we have NEITHER clang NOR llc on PATH, we need the `cc` crate approach for the MSVC fallback
design_decisions.selected_approach: Hybrid: Try clang first (single command), then fall back to cc-crate MSVC path. But since the .ll-to-.obj step requires llc or clang, we MUST ensure at least one LLVM tool is available. The pragmatic solution: require clang to be installed and provide clear error messages with install instructions.

design_decisions.choice: Require clang on PATH; provide install instructions in error message
rationale: LLVM IR text (.ll files) can only be compiled by LLVM-based tools (clang, llc, llvm-as)
MSVC cl.exe cannot compile LLVM IR directly — it only accepts C/C++ source or .obj files
gcc also cannot compile LLVM IR directly
Therefore clang (or llc + linker) is the ONLY viable path for .ll -> binary
On Windows: `winget install LLVM.LLVM` or download from https://releases.llvm.org/
On Linux: `apt install clang` or `dnf install clang`
On macOS: `xcode-select --install` (clang comes with Xcode command line tools)
This is a hard requirement, not a nice-to-have
fallback_chain: 3. Try `llc` on PATH → `llc -filetype=obj input.ll -o input.obj` then link with system linker
4. If none found → error with platform-specific install instructions

design_decisions.choice: Use std::env::temp_dir() for temp files, not the `tempfile` crate
rationale: We only need one temp file (.ll), which we write and delete ourselves
Adding `tempfile` as a dependency for one file is overkill
std::env::temp_dir() + process ID gives sufficient uniqueness
Cleanup is trivial: delete the .ll file on success, leave it on failure for debugging

design_decisions.choice: Default behavior when no --emit and no -o: compile to a.out (Unix) or a.exe (Windows)
rationale: Follows C compiler convention (clang, gcc both default to a.out)
User can always specify -o to choose output name
Intuitive for anyone familiar with compiled languages

architecture_overview.compilation_pipeline: .axm → lex → parse → HIR lower → codegen (LLVM IR text) → write temp .ll → invoke clang → native binary
data_flow: 5. Codegen → LLVM IR text string (existing, axiom-codegen)
6. Write LLVM IR to temp file: {temp_dir}/axiom_{pid}_{hash}.ll (NEW)
7. Find C compiler: search PATH for clang, clang-N, llc (NEW)
10. Report success or show compiler errors (NEW)

crates/axiom-driver/Cargo.toml — add `which` dependency for compiler discovery

files_to_create.purpose: Compiler discovery and LLVM IR to native binary compilation

detailed_spec.overview: Encapsulates the logic for finding a C/LLVM compiler on the system and invoking it to compile LLVM IR text into a native executable. Handles temp file management, compiler discovery with fallback chain, and error reporting.

public_functions.doc: Compile LLVM IR text to a native executable binary. Writes IR to a temp file, invokes clang (or fallback compiler), and cleans up.
algorithm: 2. Write llvm_ir string to temp file
3. Call find_compiler() to locate clang or llc
- Llc(llc_path): invoke `{llc_path} -filetype=obj {temp.ll} -o {temp.obj}`, then find linker and link
5. Check exit status of compiler command
6. If failure: read stderr, return error with compiler output
7. If success: delete temp .ll file (and temp .obj if applicable)
error_cases: CompilerNotFound → miette error with platform-specific install instructions
TempFileWriteFailed → miette error with path and OS error
CompilationFailed → miette error with compiler stderr output
LinkerFailed → miette error with linker stderr output

internal_functions.name: find_compiler
internal_functions.doc: Search for a suitable compiler to compile LLVM IR to native code
algorithm: 2. Try versioned clang: clang-19, clang-18, clang-17, clang-16, clang-15 → return CompilerKind::Clang(path)
4. Return Err with platform-specific install instructions

internal_functions.name: install_instructions
internal_functions.doc: Return platform-specific instructions for installing clang

returns.windows:
Install LLVM: winget install LLVM.LLVM
Or download from: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/releases
After installing, ensure clang.exe is on your PATH.
returns.macos:
Install Xcode command line tools: xcode-select --install
Or install LLVM via Homebrew: brew install llvm
returns.linux:
Install clang:
  Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install clang
  Fedora/RHEL: sudo dnf install clang
  Arch: sudo pacman -S clang

variants.name: Clang
variants.doc: clang found at this path — can compile .ll directly to executable

variants.name: Llc
variants.doc: llc found at this path — compile .ll to .obj, then link separately

clang_flags.-O2: Enable standard optimizations (LLVM mem2reg will promote our allocas to SSA)
clang_flags.-Wno-override-module: Suppress warning about overriding module target triple if host triple differs slightly

temp_file_strategy.location: std::env::temp_dir() (e.g., C:\Users\...\AppData\Local\Temp on Windows, /tmp on Unix)
temp_file_strategy.cleanup_on_success: Delete .ll file (and .obj if Llc path was used)
temp_file_strategy.cleanup_on_failure: Leave .ll file for debugging, print its path in error message

AC-1.5.1.description: axiom compile fibonacci.axm -o fib produces an executable
AC-1.5.1.implementation: The None arm in main.rs calls compile::compile_llvm_ir_to_binary() which invokes clang to produce the binary

AC-1.5.2.description: Compiled fibonacci program runs and prints correct Fibonacci(40) = 102334155
AC-1.5.2.implementation: The codegen already produces correct LLVM IR for fibonacci (verified in M1.4). clang compiles and links with libc for printf/puts support.

AC-1.5.3.description: axiom compile hello.axm -o hello produces an executable that prints greeting
AC-1.5.3.implementation: Same compilation pipeline. hello.axm uses print() which calls puts().

AC-1.5.4.implementation: Already implemented in main.rs (no changes needed)

AC-1.5.6.implementation: Already implemented in main.rs (no changes needed)

AC-1.5.7.implementation: Already implemented in main.rs (no changes needed)

AC-1.5.8.implementation: No existing tests should break. New tests should pass.

BC-1.5.1.description: Full pipeline for fibonacci.axm under 5 seconds
BC-1.5.1.analysis: Our pipeline (parse + lower + codegen) runs in milliseconds. clang -O2 on a small .ll file typically takes 100-500ms. Total well under 5 seconds.

BC-1.5.2.analysis: A minimal C program (which is essentially what our LLVM IR produces) compiles to ~10-50KB on Linux, ~50-200KB on Windows (with MSVC CRT). Well under 1MB.

BC-1.5.3.analysis: Fibonacci(40) with -O2 (which promotes allocas to registers via mem2reg) should run in microseconds. The iterative algorithm is O(n) with n=40.

implementation_order.description: Add `which` to workspace and driver Cargo.toml
Create crates/axiom-driver/src/compile.rs with compiler discovery and invocation logic
Update main.rs to use compile module for native compilation
Install clang on this system (winget install LLVM.LLVM) and verify e2e
implementation_order.note: This is a manual prerequisite step before tests can pass

risk_assessment.risk: clang not installed on the build system
risk_assessment.mitigation: Clear error message with install instructions. For CI, include clang installation in CI setup step. For this dev machine: `winget install LLVM.LLVM`.

risk_assessment.risk: LLVM IR uses printf/puts which require libc linkage
risk_assessment.mitigation: clang automatically links libc. On Windows/MSVC, clang links against msvcrt. On Linux, glibc is linked by default. This is not an issue.

risk_assessment.risk: Windows-specific path handling issues (backslashes, spaces in paths)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use std::path::PathBuf throughout. Pass paths as OsStr to Command. Test with paths containing spaces.

risk_assessment.risk: Target triple mismatch between LLVM IR and clang's default target
risk_assessment.mitigation: The LLVM IR already includes `target triple = "x86_64-pc-windows-msvc"`. clang respects this. Add -Wno-override-module to suppress any warnings.

risk_assessment.risk: LLVM IR format string `%lld` may not work on all Windows C runtimes
risk_assessment.mitigation: MSVC's printf supports %lld since VS2015. Our target (VS 18 Community) is well past this. On Linux/macOS, %lld is universally supported.

hard_requirements: clang must be available on PATH (or discoverable via versioned name like clang-18)
which crate added to Cargo.toml workspace

compile_rs_skeleton: //! Native binary compilation — invokes external C compiler on LLVM IR text.
/// The kind of compiler found on the system.
/// clang — can compile .ll directly to executable.
/// llc — compile .ll to .obj, then link separately.
/// Compile LLVM IR text to a native executable binary.
.map_err(|e| miette::miette!("failed to write temp file {}: {}", temp_ll.display(), e))?;
// Clean up temp file on success
eprintln!("note: LLVM IR written to {} for debugging", temp_ll.display());

"Install LLVM/clang:
  winget install LLVM.LLVM
  Or download from https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/releases

After installing, ensure clang.exe is on your PATH."
"Install clang:\n  xcode-select --install\n  Or: brew install llvm"

"Install clang:
  Ubuntu/Debian: sudo apt install clang
  Fedora: sudo dnf install clang
  Arch: sudo pacman -S clang"
main_rs_none_arm: return Err(miette::miette!("parsing failed with {} error(s)", result.errors.len()));
miette::miette!("HIR lowering failed with {} error(s)", errors.len())

notes: The compile.rs module is intentionally simple — no async, no caching, no incremental compilation. Those are Phase 2+ concerns.
We use eprintln for status messages (like 'compiled X -> Y') to keep stdout clean for piping.
The -Wno-override-module flag prevents clang from warning when the .ll file's target triple doesn't exactly match clang's default (e.g., x86_64-pc-windows-msvc19.0.0 vs x86_64-pc-windows-msvc).

The code reuses the parse/lower/codegen pipeline from the --emit=llvm-ir branch. Consider extracting a shared helper function to avoid duplication, but this is a minor refactor that can be done opportunistically.

On this specific Windows 11 dev machine, clang is NOT installed. The implementer must run `winget install LLVM.LLVM` before the e2e tests will pass. The architect spec accounts for this in the error handling design.
